name: Wallace Mugo Itotia
title: Full-Stack Software Developer — Systems Architecture & Observability
objective: Self-directed systems architect specializing in request lifecycle instrumentation, API contracts, and observability. Built deep expertise through deliberate practice in AOP, separation of concerns, and distributed tracing. Currently engaged in production ERP integration (Microsoft Dynamics) and zero-trust logistics platform architecture. Delivered production offline-first repair platform with thermal printing and WhatsApp integration.

featured_projects.name: Zero-Trust Offline-First Logistics Platform
highlights: 5-role hierarchical token delegation (GlobalAdmin → Member) with hardware attestation
72-hour CRDT-based offline sync with bloom filter revocation
TTLC metric replacing ETA with conservative safety margin
H3 hexagonal indexing for sub-15ms nearest-branch queries

featured_projects.name: Offline-First Repair Shop Platform
highlights: 30-second check-in with thermal QR receipt printing over TCP/IP
Offline-first operation with PDF fallback and print queue retry
Returning customer recognition via phone lookup (Stripe-grade UX)
WhatsApp notifications via Twilio on repair completion
Role-based access control with staff activity tracking and owner dashboard
One-click Windows installer with scheduled task auto-start
PWA installable as desktop app (no browser chrome)
ERP integration for Microsoft Dynamics — data mapping, batch processing, API orchestration
Direct collaboration with senior backend engineer on production architecture
Structured logging and error handling for financial transaction reconciliation

experience.company: Deep-dive: AOP · OOP · Request Lifecycle · API Contracts
highlights: AOP & Separation of Concerns: Built middleware pipelines with cross-cutting logging, validation
Request Lifecycle Instrumentation: Correlation IDs, structured logging, trace propagation
API Contract Structuring: OpenAPI-first APIs with Zod/TypeScript validation
Architecture Exploration: NestJS modules, FastAPI DI, repository pattern, DDD
Managed IT infrastructure, network systems, staff technical support
Systematic problem-solving applied to hardware/software issues
Troubleshot software/hardware/network, data organization, workflow efficiency

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
